title: Fix Foreign Key Constraint Violation on ERD View Save
description:
A SQL integrity constraint violation occurs when saving an ERD view. The error is a foreign key constraint failure on `dtp_scorecard_erd_views.object_id` referencing `dtp_scorecards.id`, indicating an attempt to insert an ERD view record with an object_id that does not exist in the scorecards table.

The error was logged in New Relic on the `saveERDView` action. After investigation, the error could not be reproduced in subsequent months and was resolved or self-correcting.
acceptance_criteria: ERD view save operation does not produce foreign key constraint violations
Object ID is validated before insert into erd_views table
middleware_changes: Validation added or data integrity check applied to prevent inserting ERD view records with invalid object_id references.
implementation_notes: Error was captured via New Relic monitoring. The issue occurred on the saveERDView transaction. After months without recurrence, the ticket was marked done. The foreign key constraint is between dtp_scorecard_erd_views.object_id and dtp_scorecards.id.
comment_summary: Developer acknowledged the issue but could not reproduce it over several months of testing. Both agreed to mark it done/withdrawn as the error had not recurred.
